LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT ROGER CARDENAS' MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MR. CARDENAS IS NOT ENTITLED TO VACATE THE JUDGMENT DUE TO CCP 473(B) SINCE THIS MOTION WAS FILED MORE THAN SIX MONTHS AFTER THE JUDGMENT WAS ENTERED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR #8189 =302/HK

calendar.matters:
ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Aug-15-2017 Discharged

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgement On Roger Cardenas
Off Calendar May-09-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Roger Cardenas' motion to vacate judgment is denied. Mr. Cardenas is not entitled to vacate the judgment due to CCP 473(b) since this motion was filed more than six months after the judgment was entered. Nor is Mr. Cardenas entitled to vacate the judgment on non-statutory equitable grounds such as extrinsic mistake because he knew about the judgment no later than October 2016, but did not file this motion until April 2017, precluding a finding that he acted diligently. (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal. 4th 975, 982 (diligence is a required element for relief based on equitable grounds)). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-29-2009 Dismissal Or Judgment On File As To All Defendants

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-19-2009 Set For Order To Show Cause On Jul-22-09 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2009 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ALEKS KHOLODENKO, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ROGER CARDENAS, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.


RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT ROGER CARDENAS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR FOR INSUFFICIENT NOTICE. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
